description_en: The eight pseudo-fawasil in سورة الأنفال are serialized into paired clusters, and the final “أمرا كان مفعولا” is identified only by an occurrence note (“الثاني بعده”).
impact_en: The eight-item extraction is reliable, but it requires editorial disaggregation of the running phrasing.

description_en: The uploaded text witness drops the final closure after “وَإِلَى الله ترجع الْأُمُور” at the end of the pseudo-fawasil sentence.
impact_en: The translation and structured extraction rely on the internal count and the checked parallel witness to understand the final identifying phrase.

description_en: The first transmitted head, “مؤمنين,” appears without a displayed numeral.
impact_en: The recovered ordinal layer treats this as head 1 while preserving the witness’s lack of a displayed numeral.

description_en: After displayed 41 “مفعولا,” the witness inserts an unnumbered “عليم” before the line displayed as 42 “الصدور.”
impact_en: The inserted head must be preserved explicitly as ordinal 43 in the recovered later-Madani sequence.

description_en: The witness ends with a blank “٧٥” line before سورة التوبة begins.
impact_en: The terminal line is preserved as a display total only and is not treated as an additional head.

description_en: Because the transmitted ruʾūs begin with an unnumbered first head, omit the expected numeral on the inserted “عليم” after displayed 41, and end with a blank total line, the displayed numerals do not independently encode the full later-Madani 76-head sequence.
impact_en: The dataset stores recovered later-Madani ordinals together with witness display numbers where present.
